{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "tested/inspected",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 90,
  "value": 4563,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 420.4,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "System failed the standing vacuum decay test",
    "Pressure rose rapidly from 359 microns to 4563 microns in 10 minutes",
    "The continuous rise well past 1000-1500 microns indicates a physical leak in the system rather than simple moisture outgassing"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Pressurize the system with dry nitrogen and a trace refrigerant gas",
    "Perform leak detection using an electronic leak detector, bubble solution, or ultrasonic detector to locate the leak",
    "Repair the identified leak (re-braze or tighten fittings), then re-test"
  ],
  "rationale": "A system that rises from 359 microns to 4563 microns in 10 minutes has a significant leak. If it were only moisture or outgassing, the pressure rise would typically level off below 1000 to 1500 microns. A continuous rise to near atmospheric pressure (or high micron levels) indicates atmospheric air is leaking into the system.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608 Evacuation Requirements",
    "Industry Standard Evacuation and Micron Decay Testing Procedures"
  ]
}
